message:
fuzz(command_option_parsers): seedable text corpus with ACCEPT/REJECT contract

Add a text-seed fast path to the fuzz_command_option_parsers
libfuzzer target plus 24 hand-curated structured seeds that
exercise deep option-parser branches across BRPOPLPUSH, EVAL,
GEOSEARCH, HSCAN, SCAN, XGROUP SETID, XREAD, XRANGE, ZRANGE,
and ZUNIONSTORE. Also add a contract test in fr-command's unit
suite that locks the corpus shape so future edits can't drift.

Why a text-seed fast path
-------------------------
The arbitrary-derived FuzzInput path mutates random bytes into
shaped command structs, which is great for finding panics but
slow at reaching subtle option-parser combinations (e.g. "WITHSCORES
must come after LIMIT but is permitted before BY"). Pre-seeding
the corpus with human-readable command argvs lets libFuzzer warm
up against known-coverage points before mutation kicks in, and
makes the seed files reviewable in PR diffs.

The fast path is opt-in: any input whose first bytes match the
literal header `FR_OPTION_SEED` is parsed as a tiny one-line text
DSL — `ACCEPT|argv...` or `REJECT|argv...`. Each line is split
on the first '|' into an expectation and a whitespace-tokenized
argv, dispatched against a freshly-seeded Store, and asserted:

- ACCEPT lines must NOT yield a rejection (Err or RespFrame::Error)
- REJECT lines MUST yield a rejection AND must leave the store
  bytewise-identical (state_digest before == after) — so the
  parser fails fast without partial mutation

Inputs that don't start with the header keep going through the
existing arbitrary-derived structured path, so coverage of the
random-mutation surface is unchanged.

The 24 seeds (in fuzz/corpus/fuzz_command_option_parsers/)
-----------------------------------------------------------
ACCEPT seeds — pin a non-trivial command argv that historically
hit a parser bug or covers a tricky branch:
  - brpoplpush_decimal_timeout    fractional timeout in BRPOPLPUSH
  - brpoplpush_nan_timeout        NaN timeout (must reject — covered
                                  by the REJECT counterpart below)
  - eval_two_keys / eval_no_keys  EVAL numkeys argument arity
  - geo_count_any_withcoord       GEOSEARCH COUNT n ANY WITHCOORD
                                  combo
  - geo_count_zero                GEOSEARCH COUNT 0 (parser must
                                  accept — Redis treats 0 as "no
                                  limit")
  - geo_desc_withdist_hash        GEOSEARCH DESC WITHDIST WITHHASH
  - hscan_match_count /
    hscan_zero_count              HSCAN MATCH/COUNT permutations
  - scan_match_count_type         SCAN with three option groups
  - xgroup_setid_explicit         XGROUP SETID with explicit id
  - xrange_bare_bounds            XRANGE without min/max sentinels
  - xread_bare_ms                 XREAD with bare ms id (no -seq)
  - zrange_limit_then_withscores  ZRANGE LIMIT then WITHSCORES (an
                                  ordering Redis 7 tightened)
  - zrange_withscores_limit       opposite ordering — also accept
  - zunionstore_weights_aggregate ZUNIONSTORE with WEIGHTS+AGGREGATE
                                  in canonical order

REJECT seeds — pin commands the parser MUST reject AND must leave
the store untouched:
  - eval_negative_numkeys         EVAL numkeys -1 (parse error)
  - geo_missing_count_value       GEOSEARCH COUNT (no value)
  - scan_missing_match            SCAN MATCH (no pattern)
  - xgroup_setid_invalid          XGROUP SETID with bad id format
  - xread_invalid_id              XREAD with malformed id
  - zrange_missing_limit_count    ZRANGE LIMIT offset (no count)
  - zunionstore_invalid_aggregate ZUNIONSTORE AGGREGATE FOO
  - zunionstore_missing_weight    ZUNIONSTORE WEIGHTS (no value)

Contract test (in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs)
-----------------------------------------------
fuzz_command_option_parsers_corpus_matches_documented_contract
walks the corpus tree at compile time via CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR,
opens each seed, asserts:

1. The first line is exactly "FR_OPTION_SEED"
2. The first non-blank, non-#-comment line is "<KIND>|argv..."
3. <KIND> matches the expected kind for the seed's filename
   (we keep an explicit (filename, expected_kind) table inside
   the test so a typo can't sneak in)
4. The argv dispatched against a fully-seeded Store actually
   honors that contract — ACCEPT seeds pass, REJECT seeds error
   AND produce no store mutation (state_digest preserved)

The seeded store carries representative scratch keys for every
data type the seeds exercise (string, hash, list, sorted set,
stream + group, geo set, source list for BRPOPLPUSH), so the
contract test runs the real dispatcher rather than a stub.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+FR_OPTION_SEED
+ACCEPT|GEOSEARCH geo FROMLONLAT 13.5 38.1 BYRADIUS 200 km COUNT 1 ANY WITHCOORD

@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+FR_OPTION_SEED
+ACCEPT|GEOSEARCH geo FROMLONLAT 13.5 38.1 BYRADIUS 200 km DESC WITHDIST WITHHASH

@@ -0,0 +1,2 @@
+FR_OPTION_SEED
+ACCEPT|ZUNIONSTORE zdest 2 zs1 zs2 WEIGHTS 2 3 AGGREGATE MIN
